differences: Layout structure differs: production uses compact, context cards/popovers; prototype uses a full-height right drawer, which changes visual balance and density.
Spacing is looser in prototype: larger paddings, taller rows, and bigger inter-section gaps than production.
Typography scale/weight is heavier in prototype; production has tighter type scale with clearer primary-vs-secondary text contrast.
Color treatment is brighter in prototype (stronger blue/green/red accents and panel gradient); production is darker and more restrained.
Border radius is less consistent in prototype: many blocks look flatter/squarer versus the rounder card and chip corners in production.
Shadows/overlays are stronger in prototype; production uses subtler depth with lighter blur and glow.
Image sizing/cropping differs: prototype avatar/hero tiles are larger and more dominant; production keeps imagery smaller relative to text blocks.
Information hierarchy is flatter in prototype because many modules have similar visual weight; production emphasizes key items more clearly.
top_3_fixes: Match production density: reduce drawer/card padding, row heights, and vertical gaps; keep a more compact panel footprint.
Recalibrate type hierarchy: shrink secondary text sizes/weights, reserve strong weight/contrast for player name and key stats only.
Tune visual style to production: reduce gradient/shadow intensity, standardize larger corner radius values, and scale down avatars/icons.
